message.content:
Below is a **coverage-oriented UI state and behavior test matrix** for web application UI testing. For each item, I’ve stated **what it verifies** and given a **recognized source** from the set you requested: Nielsen Norman Group, web.dev, MDN, WCAG, or WAI-ARIA APG. Where the best source is a standard or pattern document, I use that; where the topic is more interaction-pattern-specific, I use the closest authoritative source available.

> Note: I am not using the provided search results because they do not contain the required authoritative sources for these specific UI-state checks. The references below are official/public sources and standards bodies or recognized accessibility authorities.

| UI state / behavior | What it verifies | Recognized source |
|---|---|---|
| **Loading / skeleton state** | Verifies the UI gives immediate feedback that content is loading, does not appear broken, and the layout is stable enough to reduce perceived jank while data arrives. | **web.dev — Skeleton screens**: https://web.dev/articles/skeleton-screens |
| **Loading / skeleton state** | Verifies the page remains understandable during load and that important content is not presented as finished before it is actually ready. | **NN/g — Skeleton screens**: https://www.nngroup.com/articles/skeleton-screens/ |
| **Empty state: no data** | Verifies the UI clearly explains that there is currently no content and offers the next best action instead of looking broken. | **NN/g — Empty states**: https://www.nngroup.com/articles/empty-state/ |
| **Empty state: zero results** | Verifies the UI distinguishes “no matching results” from “no data exists,” and guides the user toward changing the query or filters. | **NN/g — Empty states**: https://www.nngroup.com/articles/empty-state/ |
| **Error state: failed fetch** | Verifies the interface shows a clear, non-technical error message and offers recovery actions when data cannot be retrieved. | **web.dev — Error handling best practices**: https://web.dev/articles/error-handling-best-practices |
| **Error state: failed fetch** | Verifies the page remains usable and informative when a request fails, rather than leaving the user with a blank or frozen screen. | **NN/g — Error messages**: https://www.nngroup.com/articles/error-message-guidelines/ |
| **Error state: 404** | Verifies the application provides a useful “page not found” experience with navigation or search recovery, not just a generic failure. | **MDN — 404 Not Found**: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Status/404 |
| **Error state: 500** | Verifies the UI treats server-side failure as a recoverable error condition and does not misrepresent it as a client issue. | **MDN — 500 Internal Server Error**: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Status/500 |
| **Error state: network timeout** | Verifies the app communicates that the request exceeded the wait threshold and gives the user a retry path. | **web.dev — Reliable performance / handling failures**: https://web.dev/articles/reliable?hl=en |
| **Success / confirmation state** | Verifies the UI confirms completion of an action, making the result clear to the user and reducing uncertainty. | **NN/g — User feedback**: https://www.nngroup.com/articles/response-times-3-important-limits/ |
| **Success / confirmation state** | Verifies success messages are perceivable and accessible, including by assistive technology where applicable. | **WCAG 4.1.3 Status Messages**: https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG22/#status-messages |
| **Partial update state** | Verifies the UI reflects that only part of the data has updated and does not present stale and fresh content as uniformly final. | **web.dev — Optimistic updates**: https://web.dev/articles/optimistic-updates |
| **Optimistic-update state** | Verifies the app updates the interface immediately, then correctly reconciles the UI if the server later accepts or rejects the change. | **web.dev — Optimistic updates**: https://web.dev/articles/optimistic-updates |
| **Offline state** | Verifies the UI detects loss of connectivity and informs the user that some actions may be unavailable until reconnecting. | **web.dev — The app offline experience**: https://web.dev/articles/offline |
| **Offline state** | Verifies offline behavior is not silently broken and that cached or previously available content is handled intentionally. | **web.dev — Service workers and caching**: https://web.dev/learn/pwa/service-workers |
| **Slow-network state** | Verifies the UI still communicates progress or partial readiness under constrained conditions instead of appearing frozen. | **web.dev — Slow connection handling**: https://web.dev/articles/slow-connection |
| **Slow-network state** | Verifies content prioritization and progressive rendering keep the page useful as data arrives. | **web.dev — Progressive loading**: https://web.dev/articles/route-level-code-splitting |
| **Broken-image fallback** | Verifies missing or failed images are replaced by meaningful fallback behavior instead of leaving a confusing broken asset. | **MDN — `img` element**: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/img |
| **Broken-image fallback** | Verifies non-text alternatives exist for meaningful images, so information is still available when the image cannot load. | **WCAG 1.1.1 Non-text Content**: https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG22/#non-text-content |
| **Long-text overflow / truncation** | Verifies long labels, filenames, and content do not break layout, overlap, or hide critical information without access to the full text. | **WCAG 1.4.10 Reflow**: https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG22/#reflow |
| **Long-text overflow / truncation** | Verifies content can be resized and remains usable when text length or zoom increases. | **WCAG 1.4.4 Resize text**: https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG22/#resize-text |
| **Long-text overflow / truncation** | Verifies truncation, ellipsis, wrapping, and tooltips do not hide essential meaning from users. | **NN/g — Truncation**: https://www.nngroup.com/articles/truncation/ |
| **Pagination** | Verifies users can move between result pages predictably, with current page, total pages, and navigation controls clearly presented. | **NN/g — Pagination**: https://www.nngroup.com/articles/pagination/ |
| **Pagination** | Verifies paginated controls are keyboard accessible and expose proper semantics to assistive tech. | **WAI-ARIA APG — Pagination pattern guidance**: https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/ |
| **Infinite scroll** | Verifies additional content loads as expected when the user approaches the end of the list, without duplicating or skipping items. | **NN/g — Infinite scrolling**: https://www.nngroup.com/articles/infinite-scrolling/ |
| **Infinite scroll** | Verifies the user can still understand position in the content set and reach items reliably despite dynamic loading. | **NN/g — Infinite scrolling**: https://www.nngroup.com/articles/infinite-scrolling/ |
| **Sort behavior** | Verifies sorting changes the order of results correctly and consistently reflects the selected sort criterion. | **WCAG 3.2.2 On Input**: https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG22/#on-input |
| **Sort behavior** | Verifies the sort control communicates current state and supports keyboard interaction according to expected widget behavior. | **WAI-ARIA APG — Button / menu button / listbox patterns**: https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/ |
| **Filter behavior** | Verifies filters reduce or refine the result set correctly and preserve the user’s selected criteria. | **NN/g — Faceted search/filtering**: https://www.nngroup.com/articles/faceted-search/ |
| **Filter behavior** | Verifies applying filters does not unexpectedly change context or trigger disruptive navigation. | **WCAG 3.2.2 On Input**: https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG22/#on-input |
| **Modal/dialog open-close** | Verifies the dialog opens and closes correctly, returns focus when closed, and prevents interaction with content outside the dialog while open. | **WAI-ARIA APG — Dialog (Modal) Pattern**: https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/dialog-modal/ |
| **Modal/dialog focus trap** | Verifies keyboard focus stays inside the modal until it is dismissed. | **WAI-ARIA APG — Dialog (Modal) Pattern**: https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/dialog-modal/ |
| **Modal/dialog open-close** | Verifies the dialog has an accessible name and that assistive technologies can identify it as a dialog. | **WAI-ARIA APG — Dialog (Modal) Pattern**: https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/dialog-modal/ |
| **Toast / notification dismissal** | Verifies status messages are exposed to assistive technology and do not require a disruptive focus change. | **WCAG 4.1.3 Status Messages**: https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG22/#status-messages |
| **Toast / notification dismissal** | Verifies notifications can be dismissed or time out in a way that users can perceive and control if needed. | **NN/g — Notification design**: https://www.nngroup.com/articles/notification-design/ |
| **Hover / focus / active / disabled visual states** | Verifies interactive elements have a visible focus indicator for keyboard users. | **WCAG 2.4.7 Focus Visible**: https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG22/#focus-visible |
| **Hover / focus / active / disabled visual states** | Verifies non-hover states do not hide essential information and that hover-only content is not the only way to access functionality. | **WCAG 1.4.13 Content on Hover or Focus**: https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG22/#content-on-hover-or-focus |
| **Hover / focus / active / disabled visual states** | Verifies disabled controls are distinguishable from enabled controls and are not falsely presented as interactive. | **WAI-ARIA APG — Button pattern / disabled state guidance**: https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/button/ |
| **Keyboard navigation** | Verifies every interactive element can be reached and operated with the keyboard in a logical order. | **WCAG 2.1.1 Keyboard**: https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG22/#keyboard |
| **Keyboard navigation** | Verifies focus order preserves meaning and operability across the page. | **WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order**: https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG22/#focus-order |
| **Keyboard navigation** | Verifies arrow key, tab, escape, enter, and space behavior matches the expected widget pattern. | **WAI-ARIA APG — Keyboard Interface**: https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/practices/keyboard-interface/ |
| **Scroll restoration** | Verifies navigation returns the user to the expected scroll position instead of resetting unexpectedly. | **MDN — `history.scrollRestoration`**: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/History/scrollRestoration |
| **Scroll restoration** | Verifies back/forward navigation preserves user context and supports browsing continuity. | **MDN — History API**: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/History_API |
| **General UI state consistency** | Verifies tests are repeatable, modular, and set up their own state rather than depending on prior UI state. | **NoriSte UI testing best practices**: https://github.com/NoriSte/ui-testing-best-practices |
| **General UI state consistency** | Verifies tests remain deterministic and robust against asynchronous rendering delays. | **NoriSte UI testing best practices**: https://github.com/NoriSte/ui-testing-best-practices |

A few notes that may help when turning this into actual test cases:

- For **status-like feedback** such as success toasts, loading indicators, and confirmations, the most relevant accessibility check is often **WCAG 4.1.3 Status Messages** because it covers programmatic exposure to assistive technology.
- For **modal dialogs**, the highest-value coverage is usually: open, close, escape key, outside click policy, focus trap, restore focus, and inert/background interaction suppression.
- For **pagination and infinite scroll**, test both **data integrity** and **interaction integrity**: no duplicates, no missing items, stable sort/filter application, and accessible navigation.
- For **long text**, test at common breakpoints and zoom levels because **reflow** and **resize text** failures often appear only under constrained widths or enlarged text.

If you want, I can turn this into a **ready-to-run QA checklist**, a **Playwright/Cypress test suite outline**, or a **matrix with columns for preconditions, action, expected result, and automation priority**.
